operating_state: Revenue reached $6.51 billion, up 68% year-over-year driven by Gaming segment growth from GeForce RTX 30 Series Ampere architecture products and Data Center growth from Ampere architecture ramp into vertical industries and hyperscale customers. Professional Visualization revenue surged from Ampere architecture GPU adoption while Automotive revenue increased reflecting pandemic recovery. CMP cryptocurrency mining products contributed to OEM revenue growth.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded 600 basis points year-over-year to 64.8%, primarily due to absence of prior year's Mellanox acquisition inventory step-up expense and higher average selling prices in desktop GeForce GPUs from high-end Ampere architecture product mix. Operating expenses increased driven by employee additions, higher compensation costs including stock-based compensation, and infrastructure investments.
material_events: Paid $100 million in quarterly cash dividends and maintained $7.24 billion share repurchase authorization through December 2022 without executing repurchases during the quarter. Issued $5 billion in debt during the period contributing to cash position increase to $19.65 billion. Repaid $1 billion Notes Due 2021 in August 2021 following quarter-end.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continues creating supply chain constraints and component availability limitations requiring advance inventory orders, premium payments and deposits to secure capacity. Cryptocurrency market volatility impacts GPU demand with majority of Ampere GeForce shipments being Low Hash Rate variants to direct gaming versus mining usage. Geographic revenue concentration outside United States increased substantially from prior year.
forward_outlook: Management expects to remain supply-constrained beyond second half of fiscal 2022 and continues placing non-cancellable inventory orders in advance of normal lead times. Arm acquisition regulatory process ongoing across multiple jurisdictions with discussions taking longer than initially anticipated, though management maintains confidence in deal rationale and regulatory approval prospects.
uncertainty_profile: Arm acquisition faces regulatory review across multiple jurisdictions with some licensees expressing concerns, creating potential for $1.25 billion signing consideration refund if terminated under certain circumstances. Cryptocurrency mining demand volatility and pending Ethereum standard changes may impact GPU aftermarket resales and new product demand. COVID-19 pandemic evolution affects customer demand timing and supply chain availability with material negative impact potential.
